headline: Statutory bodies' reserves total RM280 billion with minimal oversight
context: Malaysia's 147 statutory bodies hold combined reserves of RM280 billion, operating under varying levels of oversight with many not subject to regular parliamentary scrutiny.

cards.big: 147 statutory bodies hold RM280 billion — many escape regular parliamentary review
cards.sub: Combined reserves exceed annual federal government revenue

cards.big: Each statutory body operates under its own enabling act with different governance standards
cards.sub: No uniform governance code applies across all 147 entities

cards.big: RM280 billion in reserves generates estimated RM14 billion in annual investment income
cards.sub: Investment decisions affecting billions happen without consistent oversight framework

cards.big: Only 89 of 147 statutory bodies submit annual reports to Parliament on time
cards.sub: 58 entities — 39% — routinely miss parliamentary reporting deadlines

cards.big: RM280 billion managed under 147 different governance standards is not a system — it's chaos
Should all statutory bodies follow a single governance code with uniform reporting standards?
